vulnerability.title: Verify User Who Owns /var/log Directory
vulnerability.description: To properly set the owner of /var/log, run the command: $ sudo chown root /var/log
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: newown="" if id "0" >/dev/null 2>&1; then newown="0" fi if [[ -z "$newown" ]]; then >&2 echo "0 is not a defined user on the system" else find -P /var/log/ -maxdepth 0 -type d ! -user 0 -exec chown --no-dereference "$newown" {} \; fi

triage_decision.reason: The remediation only changes the owner of the /var/log directory to root, which is a standard file permission fix. It does not affect authentication, networking, or critical services, and follows typical STIG hardening practices.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: filesystems affected; Incorrect ownership could allow unauthorized modification of log files; Potential for mis‑execution if the command is run with an incorrect user ID
